title: Day 2 §3.1 item 3 PASS on live deploy — both hosts, commit ab00aefb
content: Validator session captured live wscat evidence at 2026-05-03T00:06-00:09Z (see journal/evidence-day2-wscat.txt Section C). Tested commit ab00aefb45aed94cd6fa773e24526770b5fa29fa (the bugbot-fixes head of PR #14, NOT the pre-bugbot 14fca48). Cloudflare Workers Builds bot comment in PR #14 attests deployment of ab00aefb at 2026-05-02T23:43Z (build 446911ca-e7f0-4ae7-bb47-7a2e20d42123); PR merged into main as 5f01e0e at 2026-05-02T23:56:17Z. Validation ran ~23-26 minutes after that promotion. Pre-conditions verified: GET /healthz returned 200 + JSON {ok,host,ts} on both ams.klappy.dev and ams.truthkit.ai with host echoed correctly. Test C.1 — wscat against ams.klappy.dev: ian listener received exactly four frames in protocol order (joined, stream_joined for klappy-stream-c, token data='hello-from-klappy-LIVE' broker ts 2026-05-03T00:06:41.767Z, stream_left); klappy sender received exactly one frame (his own joined echo with self_subscribe:false and peers=[ian-stream]) — zero token frames, D0009 structural self-exclusion observed on the wire. Test C.4 — same shape against ams.truthkit.ai: identical four-frame sequence on listener side (data='hello-from-truthkit', broker ts 2026-05-03T00:09:10.249Z), zero token frames on sender side. SPEC.md §3.1 item 3 is operationally attested at the production-deploy layer for both hosts. Items 4 and 5 (MCP wrapper) remain Day 3 scope and were intentionally not exercised.

title: Both bugbot regression fixes verified live — Unicode metadata + account-aware 4004
content: Test C.2 (Unicode metadata round-trip) is the wire-level regression for both bugbot UTF-8 fixes: 35f396f's btoa→utf8ToBase64 swap on the Worker→DO join-payload header path AND ab00aefb's atob→base64ToUtf8 swap inside handleConnect for X-AMS-Stream-Metadata. Connected klappy-unicode with X-AMS-Stream-Metadata: eyJkaXNwbGF5X25hbWUiOiLpq5jmqYsg8J+miiJ9 (base64 of {display_name:'高橋 🦊'}, UTF-8 bytes e9 ab 98 e6 a9 8b 20 f0 9f a6 8a). Result: 101 Switching Protocols (no HTTP 500/wire 4500); klappy.joined.metadata = {display_name:'高橋 🦊'} verbatim; ian.stream_joined.metadata = same verbatim. Byte-identical round-trip on both emitter-self and peer paths. Test C.3 (same-account stream-name reuse) is the wire-level regression for ab00aefb's account_id ownership predicate. Three concurrent connections on conv_01KQNJMKKDJ1N155WRT7 driven via node ws@8 to capture exact close codes: A=klappy/klappy-twin held 6s; B=SAME account klappy with SAME stream-name klappy-twin while A still open; C=DIFFERENT account ian with same stream-name. B got a normal joined frame at t=150ms with peers=[A] and closed cleanly at t=2519ms with code 1005 — NO 4004, ab00aefb regression observed. C closed at t=289ms with code 4004 reason='stream_name_conflict' — cross-account PROTOCOL.md §6 ownership-aware conflict still enforced (the bugbot fix narrowed the predicate, did not disable the rejection). A observed B's existence as a normal peer (stream_joined at t=756ms, stream_left at t=3131ms) — the DO is willing to register multiple streams under the same name PER account.

title: D0011 host parity attested at WebSocket-plane layer (not just control-plane)
content: Day 1 closeout attested D0011 at the control-plane layer (mint succeeds on both hosts, host-aware magic_link). Day 2 validation extends that attestation to the stream plane: test C.4 ran the full SPEC §3.1 item 3 wscat sequence against ams.truthkit.ai with the same wire shape and same per-frame outcomes as test C.1 against ams.klappy.dev. Magic link minted with host=ams.truthkit.ai (request.headers.get('host') wins as expected), WebSocket upgrade succeeded against the truthkit Custom Domain, joined/stream_joined/token/stream_left flowed in identical order with identical structural-exclusion behavior. Conclusion: the dual-host single-Worker architecture is wire-portable end-to-end, not just control-plane-portable. Cross-host conversation RESOLUTION (mint a magic_link on klappy.dev and connect via truthkit.ai) was NOT exercised in this session — that is a separate property and was not in the operator's scope. Evidence file Section C contains the full transcripts for both hosts side by side.

title: Disposition: ACCEPT — Day 2 closes; Day 2 PR (merged as 5f01e0e) is the closing artifact
content: All four operator-specified validation tests passed: SPEC §3.1 item 3 baseline (C.1), Unicode-metadata bugbot regression (C.2), same-account-reconnect bugbot regression (C.3), and D0011 host-parity re-run (C.4). All four PROTOCOL.md §4 frame types were observed in the correct order under at least one test (joined, stream_joined, token, stream_left); PROTOCOL.md §6 close code 4004 was observed under the cross-account-only condition. SPEC §3.3 Definition of Done components met: change description (the Day 2 build journal 2026-05-02-day2-conversation-do-websocket.tsv plus PR #14 + cursoragent fix commits 35f396f and ab00aefb), verification performed (this entry plus Section C of evidence-day2-wscat.txt), observed behavior (all four tests PASS, including the bugbot regressions), evidence produced (the live transcripts in Section C), self-audit (this row plus the constraint and learning rows). No iterate, no pivot. Per the operator's hard rules, Day 3 (MCP wrapper + examples/two-agents/) MUST NOT start in this session, and the four v1.1.2 doc-accuracy items enumerated in the Day 2 build journal C-row were NOT touched in this session.

title: Four v1.1.2 doc-accuracy items still open — re-flagged for the next doc-patch PR
content: Re-stating from the Day 2 build journal C-row so the items don't evaporate across the validation handoff: (1) worker/wrangler.toml lines 23-25 still reference 'wrangler secret put' which SPEC v1.1.1 and the Day 1 build journal both forbid against production. (2) SPEC.md title line 1 still reads '# AMS Spec / PRD — v1.1' while the Version line reads '1.1.1' (and §14 says title is a provenance handle that should match). (3) POC-INFRA.md §8 does not document the Workers Builds 'Root directory = worker' setting that bit Day 1's first deploy attempt. (4) SPEC.md §6 architecture diagram still has 'GET /v1/.../connect' in its connect line, which contradicts §3.1 item 3's binding 'magic link with /connect appended' under the §3.2 magic-link shape (no /v1/) that the live deploy actually returns and that this validation actually exercised. None blocks Day 3; all four should fold into a single SPEC v1.1.2 + POC-INFRA + wrangler.toml patch PR. This validation session deliberately did NOT touch any of these per the operator's hard rule.

title: wscat 6.1.0 does not surface WebSocket close codes on stdout — node ws driver needed for close-code regression tests
content: Test C.3 initially used wscat for connection C (cross-account ian/klappy-twin) and got an empty log with no close-code information — wscat reports no JSON frame because the server's close happens immediately after the 101 upgrade, and wscat's per-message log surface does not print the close code/reason at the level needed to assert against PROTOCOL.md §6. The Day 2 build journal had already noted wscat's stdin-close oddity (workaround: pipe ( sleep N ) to keep stdin open); this is a SECOND wscat oddity. Workaround: drive the connection via a small node ws@8 script that registers ws.on('close', (code, reason) => ...) — that surfaces the exact code+reason pair. The script /tmp/wstest/test3-codes.mjs (preserved in the evidence as the inline transcript headed '===== A klappy/klappy-twin =====' etc.) is the reusable pattern. Implication for Day 3+ smoke runner: any future automated smoke that needs to assert on PROTOCOL.md §6 close codes should use a node ws driver for those specific assertions; wscat remains fine for frame-content assertions where the client closes itself.

title: Magic link → WebSocket connect URL: insert /connect BEFORE the query string, not after
content: First wscat attempt in test C.1 produced 404 because the URL transform was 'append /connect to the literal magic_link' — which puts /connect AFTER the ?t=... query string, yielding a path the Worker has no route for. The correct transform: split on '?' and insert /connect between the path and the query, i.e. shell sed 's|?|/connect?|'. The PROTOCOL.md §4.1 / §3.2 path shape is /{ns}/conversations/{alias}/connect?t=... — the permissive token must remain as a query parameter. SPEC §3.1 item 3 phrases it as 'the magic link with /connect appended' which is correct only if 'appended' is read as 'inserted between path and query' rather than 'concatenated to the literal URL'. Worth noting in the Day 3+ MCP wrapper documentation and any developer-facing example client that the transform is non-trivial — clients should NOT do naive string concatenation.

title: Day 2 closed; Day 3 (MCP wrapper + examples/two-agents/) clear to start in fresh session
content: Per AGENTS.md mode-discipline ('Validation requires a context break — same agent in same session cannot honestly validate its own just-produced work'), Day 3 MUST NOT start in this validation session. Day 3 scope per POC-PLAN.md §2 'Day 3 — Monday Morning': stream ownership enforcement at the wire (only the owning account may emit on a stream — currently the worker accepts any token frame on the connection, the connection's account_id binding needs to be enforced server-side), MCP edge wrapper (the SessionDO + ams_create_conversation/ams_join/ams_send/ams_set_metadata/ams_leave/ams_recv tool surface from POC-INFRA.md §3 and SPEC.md §4 'six MCP tools'), examples/two-agents/ runnable two-agent demo. Day 3 starts in a new session that opens cold, reads SPEC.md §3.1 (items 4 and 5 are the binding tests), POC-PLAN.md §2 'Day 3', POC-INFRA.md §3 (MCP edge wrapper), and this validation closeout. The four v1.1.2 doc-accuracy items above are NOT Day 3 scope — they belong in their own dedicated patch PR after Day 3 ships.

This validation produces no code changes — only the journal artifacts (Section C of journal/evidence-day2-wscat.txt and this closeout TSV journal/2026-05-02-day2-validation-closeout.tsv).
